title: Storytelling
puzzle: Why do stories stay with us when plain information fades?
insight: Stories combine fact, emotion, sequence, and meaning in ways the mind can carry easily, which makes them powerful tools for memory and identity.
key_ideas: Shared stories shape how groups understand themselves.
mind_blower: A story is one of humanity’s oldest technologies for moving ideas between minds.
next_curiosity: If stories shape identity, how much of what you believe comes from stories you inherited?
narration_text: Why do stories stay with us when plain information fades? Stories combine fact, emotion, sequence, and meaning in ways the mind can carry easily, which makes them powerful tools for memory and identity. Narratives help information feel memorable. Stories preserve values across generations. Shared stories shape how groups understand themselves. A story is one of humanity’s oldest technologies for moving ideas between minds. If stories shape identity, how much of what you believe comes from stories you inherited?
